articles.title: Study links semaglutide and tirzepatide to fewer anxiety and depression diagnoses in obesity care
articles.description: A US electronic health record study found that adults with obesity who started semaglutide or tirzepatide had lower recorded hazards of anxiety and depression than matched patients using naltrexone-bupropion. However, among adults without type 2 diabetes, tirzepatide was linked to higher recorded hazards of anxiety and insomnia than semaglutide, underscoring the need for cautious monitoring.

articles.title: Chatham-Kent health unit warns of insufficient disinfection practices at unlicensed tattoo provider
articles.description: Chatham-Kent’s health unit has issued a warning to residents about a tattoo provider in Wallaceburg that is unlicensed and uninspected. articles.title: ETF Edge on how SpaceX may be setting a new precedent for other incoming mega IPOs
articles.description: SpaceX is joining the highly tracked Nasdaq 100 Index next week. It’s already part of the Russell 1000 Index. That means more ETFs have to buy the stock to stay aligned with the indexes, and is increasing investors’ exposure to the company . How is this changing the ETF space? And what does it mean for other big IPOS in the pipeline, like Anthropic and Open AI? FTSE Russell head of equity and multi-asset indices Arne Noack and Kathmere Capital CIO Nick Ryder join Dominic Chu on “ETF Edge” to dive in to this and much more.

articles.title: Researchers use AI to predict rare cancer immunotherapy responses
articles.description: Researchers from The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center demonstrated that an artificial intelligence (AI)-based analysis of tumor biopsies can predict responses to immunotherapy in a study of patients with rare cancers, published in the Journal for ImmunoTherapy of Cancer.

articles.title: People with strong chest and back less likely to have a heart attack, analysis suggests

articles.title: Moobs linked to ‘58% higher risk of heart attack in 10 years’ in ‘fascinating’ study
articles.description: MOOBS may be an early indicator of future heart attack, a new study warns. On the contrary, a strong chest and back are linked to a lower risk, as well as lower odds of an early grave. People with toned pecs, backs and torsos were less likely to die within the next decade, researchers said....
